contested_rolls.overview: When a skill is actively resisted by an opponent, use a Contested Roll. Two types: Opposed Rolls and Differential Rolls.

opposed_rolls.description: Both participants roll respective skills. Winner gains the better level of success. If same level, winner has the highest dice roll still within skill range.

resolution.rule: Better level wins. Same level: higher roll within range wins.
resolution.both_fail: Either re-roll with dramatic tension, or GM narrates circumstances fitting the failure.

examples_table.opposing_skills: Commerce vs Commerce, Influence vs Commerce
Athletics vs Athletics, Athletics vs Endurance, Ride vs Ride
Deceit vs Insight, Sleight vs Perception
Passion vs Passion, Passion vs Insight, Passion vs Influence
Evade vs Athletics, Evade vs Mechanisms, Evade vs Ride
Stealth vs Perception, Stealth vs Track
Influence vs Willpower, Deceit vs Willpower
Endurance vs Endurance, Endurance vs Brawn
Brawn vs Brawn, Brawn vs Endurance
Willpower vs Willpower, Seduction vs Willpower

differential_rolls.description: Both participants roll against respective skills. No outright winner — the difference in success levels determines advantage. Primarily used for combat resolution.

results_table.note: Read as Protagonist result vs Antagonist result. Value is who benefits and by how many levels.

skills_over_100.rule: If the highest skilled participant exceeds 100%, subtract the difference between 100 and their skill from ALL participants (including themselves).
skills_over_100.note: Calculate after circumstance modifiers are applied.
skills_over_100.quick_hack: Round penalty up to nearest 10% for speed. E.g., skill 113% → subtract 20% from everyone.

team_roll.description: Success is co-operative. Roll once for the highest skill in the team. Success = everyone succeeds. Failure = everyone fails.
team_roll.reverse: For situations where one failure ruins it for all, roll for the lowest skill instead.

sorting_roll.description: Success is individual. GM makes one roll. Those whose skill equals or exceeds the roll succeed; those below fail.

proportional_success.description: For large groups, the skill percentage equals the proportion of the group that succeeds. E.g., Athletics 75 → 75 out of 100 soldiers succeed.

augmenting_skills.description: One skill can aid another. The augmenting skill increases the primary skill by twice its Critical range (i.e., 20% of the augmenting skill value).
rules: Only one augmenting skill at a time
Augmenting skill must be appropriate to circumstances (GM decides)
Critical and Fumble ranges stay based on unaugmented primary skill
Characters can augment other characters' skills to assist them
A character being aided cannot also augment themselves
augmenting_skills.example: Locale 33% augments Ride 38% → adds 7% (20% of 33) → Ride becomes 45%. Critical still at 4%.

capping_skills.description: For a particular roll, the primary skill is restricted to the value of a secondary skill (if lower). Used when full ability is limited by unusual circumstances.
capping_skills.example: Lore (Strategy) 48% capped by Language (Korathian) 23% → rolls at 23%.
